questions.question: Match each mandatory assignment to its purpose.
categories: Sets the RQ, hypothesis, design, and planned analysis
Clears the study and consent before data collection
Declares any GenAI or tooling used
Full write-up following the example papers' structure

correct_mapping.Research proposal: Sets the RQ, hypothesis, design, and planned analysis
correct_mapping.Ethical Standards Compliance: Clears the study and consent before data collection
correct_mapping.Technology Statement: Declares any GenAI or tooling used
correct_mapping.Final paper: Full write-up following the example papers' structure

questions.explanation: The proposal fixes the RQ and plan, ethics compliance clears data collection, the Technology Statement discloses tooling, and the final paper is the full write-up.

questions.question: Match each course component to what it provides.
categories: Whole-cohort teaching on RQ, design, analysis, and writing
Small-group meetings with an assigned supervisor and topic
Proposal feedback, paper structure, and common pitfalls

correct_mapping.Plenary lectures: Whole-cohort teaching on RQ, design, analysis, and writing
correct_mapping.Supervision sessions: Small-group meetings with an assigned supervisor and topic
correct_mapping.Writing support sessions: Proposal feedback, paper structure, and common pitfalls

questions.explanation: Plenary lectures teach the cohort, supervision sessions are small-group topic meetings, and writing support sessions give feedback on proposals, structure, and pitfalls.

questions.question: Match each assessment element to its weight and product type.
categories: 40% of the grade and the group product
60% of the grade and the individual product

correct_mapping.Paper: 40% of the grade and the group product
correct_mapping.Exam: 60% of the grade and the individual product

questions.explanation: The paper is 40% and is the group product; the exam is 60% and is the individual product, and both must pass.

questions.question: Match each track or framework to what it concerns.
categories: Example proposals focused on human-participant methods
Example proposals focused on ML evaluation methods
Framework for handling free-riders in a team

correct_mapping.Human-Studies (HS) track: Example proposals focused on human-participant methods
correct_mapping.Machine-Learning (ML) track: Example proposals focused on ML evaluation methods
correct_mapping.Hitchhikers and Couch Potatoes: Framework for handling free-riders in a team

questions.explanation: The HS and ML tracks have different example proposals reflecting their method focus, while Hitchhikers and Couch Potatoes is the free-rider framework.

questions.question: Match each resource to its description.
categories: Reference for writing the literature review
Structural templates for the final write-up

correct_mapping.Belcher book: Reference for writing the literature review
correct_mapping.Example papers: Structural templates for the final write-up

questions.explanation: The Project Schedule Template is the required xlsx planner, Belcher's book is the lit-review reference, and example papers serve as structural templates for the write-up.

questions.question: Match each minimum project requirement to its threshold or tool.
questions.explanation: Projects need at least 2 conditions, at least 20 participants per condition, and analysis in R or Python.
